title: Proof Techniques, Invariants, and Recurrence Bounds · Spiral Review
summary: Review a proposed optimization whose runtime and correctness claims lack evidence. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a formal algorithm review packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.
Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Compare input sizes 60, 120, and 240; define the counted operation, predict the growth ratio, then challenge the claim with a reversed or duplicate-heavy input.

Inspect the Lower Bounds trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Induction correctness-and-cost proof for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Induction assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Lower Bounds: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Compare input sizes 30, 60, and 120; define the counted operation, predict the growth ratio, then challenge the claim with a reversed or duplicate-heavy input.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Argue comparison or information lower bounds without confusing them with one implementation.
